Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices surge 10% as US-Israel strikes on Iran rattle global markets
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices jumped by as much as 10 per cent as markets opened in Asia, after US and Israeli strikes on Iran heightened fears of a wider conflict in the Middle East and potential disruption to global energy supplies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Middle East War and the Return of Global Economic Risk
Finance & Economy.detail: An open confrontation between Israel, backed by the United States, and Iran has erupted into direct military exchanges, sending shockwaves through financial markets, with oil prices jumping and safe-haven assets rallying. The conflict introduces uncertainty that could deepen weakening growth momentum and fragile disinflation trends, impacting energy, financial markets, and global trade logistics.

Finance & Economy.headline: Middle East Shockwaves: How US–Israeli Strikes on Iran Could Hit Aotearoa's Economy
Finance & Economy.detail: Joint military strikes by the United States and Israel on Iran are triggering volatility in global oil markets and raising concerns about the vulnerability of trade-dependent economies like New Zealand. The economic consequences could rapidly affect Aotearoa through fuel prices, shipping costs, and broader financial markets.

Finance & Economy.headline: WTK on profitability path
Finance & Economy.detail: WTK Holdings Bhd has returned to profitability for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25), with a net profit of RM38.6mil on revenue of RM577.1mil after ceasing loss-making plywood manufacturing and scaling down on non-profitable logging operations. In 4Q25, the food segment's revenue jumped by 32% to RM39.4mil, driving its pre-tax profit higher at RM2.13mil.

Finance & Economy.headline: SMU and Fudan Launch Region's First Tech-Focused DBA
Finance & Economy.detail: Singapore Management University (SMU) and Fudan University have jointly launched the SMU-Fudan Doctor of Business Administration (Technology), a program to prepare senior leaders to navigate the intersection of business and technology. The program equips experienced executives with practice-driven doctoral research training focused on real-world technological challenges.

Technology.headline: I tried Honor's ultra-thin foldable phone at MWC 2026, and it's almost too powerful
Technology.detail: Hands-on with Honor's latest foldable phone at MWC 2026, highlighting its powerful capabilities and ultra-thin design.

Technology.headline: This gimbal-stabilized camera phone is the most bizarre thing I've seen at MWC so far
Technology.detail: A unique camera phone featuring gimbal stabilization is making waves at MWC 2026 for its unusual design and functionality.

Technology.headline: Best of MWC 2026: Live updates on phones, concepts, and robots we're seeing
Technology.detail: Live coverage from MWC 2026 showcasing the best phones, innovative concepts, and impressive robots on display.

Technology.headline: Experity AI Care Agent Helps Cut Admin Workload in Urgent Care
Technology.detail: Experity's AI Care Agent automates routine follow-ups and reduces call volume in urgent care clinics, saving 7,000 staff hours.

Technology.headline: Hitachi bets on industrial expertise to win the physical AI race
Technology.detail: Hitachi is leveraging its industrial expertise to gain an advantage in the competitive field of physical AI applications.

Science.headline: Scientists Discover the Genetic Switch That Keeps Your Organs Healthy
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered a powerful genetic switch that helps some of the body's most important immune cells grow up properly and keep our organs healthy.

Science.headline: Massive Asteroid Impact 6.3 Million Years Ago Left Giant Glass Field in Brazil
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered a vast field of tektites in Brazil — mysterious glassy fragments forged when a powerful extraterrestrial object slammed into Earth about 6.3 million years ago.

Science.headline: Beyond Amyloid Plaques: AI Reveals Hidden Chemical Changes Across the Alzheimer's Brain
Science.detail: Scientists at Rice University have produced the first full, dye-free molecular atlas of an Alzheimer's brain, uncovering chemical changes by combining laser-based imaging with machine learning.

Science.headline: New Iron Nanomaterial Wipes Out Cancer Cells Without Harming Healthy Tissue
Science.detail: Scientists at Oregon State University have engineered a powerful new nanomaterial that zeroes in on cancer cells and destroys them from the inside out.

Science.headline: A Faint Cosmic Hum Could Solve the Universe's Expansion Mystery
Science.detail: A faint cosmic hum may hold the key to unlocking the mysteries surrounding the universe's expansion.

Health.headline: Adjuvant Belzutifan-Pembrolizumab Significantly Boosts DFS in Kidney Cancer
Health.detail: Combination cut risk of recurrence or death by 28% in patients at high risk of relapse.

Health.headline: Second Biologic Might Improve Tolerability of Multi-Food Allergy Immunotherapy
Health.detail: Phase II data suggest fewer GI adverse effects when given a sequence of biologics.

Health.headline: Three in four women unaware menopause can trigger new mental illness, poll finds
Health.detail: A recent poll indicates that a significant majority of women are not aware that menopause can lead to the onset of new mental health issues.

Health.headline: The decline in healthy life expectancy in Britain should shock us all
Health.detail: A concerning trend reveals a decrease in healthy life expectancy within Britain, signaling potential systemic issues.

Health.headline: UK health official recuses himself from puberty blockers trial after bias claims
Health.detail: A UK health official has stepped aside from a puberty blockers trial following allegations of bias, ensuring impartiality in the research process.

Sports.headline: Toronto Sceptres ride early goals to 2-1 victory over Vancouver Goldeneyes
Sports.detail: Laura Messier scored her first Professional Women's Hockey League goal, lifting the Toronto Sceptres to a 2-1 victory over the Goldeneyes in Vancouver.

Sports.headline: Grizzlies center Zach Edey headed for another operation on his ailing left ankle
Sports.detail: Zach Edey, center for the Grizzlies, is scheduled for another surgery on his problematic left ankle.

Sports.headline: MLB pitcher Merrill Kelly says California tax rate swayed decision to reject Padres' free agency offer
Sports.detail: Merrill Kelly cited California's tax rate as a factor in his decision to decline a free agency offer from the San Diego Padres.

Sports.headline: Samson's Redemption Arc Puts India Past WI And Into T20 World Cup Semis
Sports.detail: Sanju Samson's performance propelled India past the West Indies and into the T20 World Cup Semis.

Sports.headline: 'My heart almost stopped' Arteta praises Raya for vital saves in Arsenal win
Sports.detail: Arteta commended Raya's crucial saves during Arsenal's victory, expressing how vital they were to the win.

Entertainment.headline: Brit Awards 2026: The surprise performers, tributes, and bits you might not have seen
Entertainment.detail: The Brit Awards 2026 took place, featuring surprise performances and heartfelt tributes.

Entertainment.headline: Actor Awards 2026: 'One Battle After Another,' 'Sinners' face off in final pre-Oscars showdown
Entertainment.detail: With two weeks to go until the Academy Awards, the Actor Awards will be the final pre-Oscars showdown for "One Battle After Another" and "Sinners."

Entertainment.headline: Choi Mina-soo announces her full-fledged entry into the entertainment industry
Entertainment.detail: Choi Mina-soo from "Solo Hell 5" announced her intention to work in broadcasting after graduating from university, expressing interest in acting, MC roles, and entertainment shows. She also addressed conflicts with other cast members and her experience on the show.

Entertainment.headline: Tina Fey 'hosting the launch' of Saturday Night Live UK
Entertainment.detail: Sky announced Saturday Night Live UK in April 2025, with Tina Fey hosting the launch, marking the first time an American show is adapted for the UK.

Entertainment.headline: Rajpal Yadav hits back at Priyadarshan's 'poor education' remark following arrest in cheque bounce case
Entertainment.detail: Rajpal Yadav responded to Priyadarshan's remark about his 'poor education' after his arrest in a cheque bounce case, stating that any judgements from them don't hold value.

Crime & Justice.headline: Repeat offender kills 2 deputies days after $50K bond despite long violent record: police
Crime & Justice.detail: A violent repeat offender with a long criminal history shot and killed two Missouri deputies before being killed in a 30-minute shoot-out with law enforcement, authorities said.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspected people smuggling boss arrested in Birmingham
Crime & Justice.detail: A suspected people smuggling boss has been arrested in Birmingham as part of a joint investigation involving the National Crime Agency and West Midlands Police.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former imam found guilty of multiple rapes and sexual assaults
Crime & Justice.detail: A former Imam has been convicted today (Friday 13 February 2026) of numerous non-consensual sexual attacks on women and girls in East London.

Crime & Justice.headline: 'Violent bully' who broke partner's neck and left her paralysed jailed for 16 years
Crime & Justice.detail: A man described as a 'violent bully' was sentenced to 16 years in jail for breaking his partner's neck and leaving her paralyzed.

Crime & Justice.headline: Beating by Guards, Not a Heart Attack, Killed Man in Mississippi Prison, Report Shows
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI is now investigating after a report showed that Mississippi guards beat an incarcerated person to death.

Education.headline: New facilities Inspire students at Batemans Bay High
Education.detail: New commercial cookery and science facilities are inspiring students at Batemans Bay High, allowing the school to deliver a Certificate II program and providing upgraded laboratories for future scientists and technicians. Students appreciate the new science laboratory, complete with a fume hood, and find the new rooms reflective of real-world kitchens, motivating them to start working.

Education.headline: New Courses Surge as Thousands Begin 2026 Studies
Education.detail: Southern Cross University is experiencing strong demand for career-focused courses, launching new offerings for 2026 and welcoming over 5,000 commencing students across its campuses and online. The success of these new courses demonstrates the University's commitment to aligning education with regional workforce needs, with the Bachelor of Education being among the most in-demand.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today (March 02): Top National, Business News, Sports News, Education News & World News with Weather Updates & Thought of the Day
Education.detail: The University of Birmingham has announced scholarships for Indian postgraduates enrolling in 2026 to support tuition and living costs. Several states in India, including Uttar Pradesh, have declared school closures until March 4 for Holi, and students and parents are advised to follow local notifications for exact schedules.

Education.headline: Non-education grads invited to apply as MOE opens 20,000 DG9 contract teacher positions from 2pm today
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education (MOE) in Malaysia has announced the opening of 20,000 new positions for Education Service Officers Grade DG9 on a contract basis in primary schools, starting from 2pm today. This recruitment is part of the ministry's preparations for the Malaysia Education Plan (RPM) 2026–2035 and aims to prepare for the voluntary enrolment of six-year-olds into Year 1 starting next year.

Education.headline: Today News Headlines for School Assembly, March 2, 2026: Indian Army intercepts Pak drone in Poonch, CBSE postpones exams due to Iran-Israel war
Education.detail: Key updates for school assemblies on March 2, 2026, include a drone incursion along the LoC, a major GST evasion bust in Uttar Pradesh, and Indian students impacted by airspace disruptions due to the ongoing Iran – Israel war. Due to the current conflict between Iran and Israel, the CBSE (Central Board of Secondary Education) has decided to postpone the class 10 and 12 exams that were scheduled for March 2 in the Middle East.

Lifestyle.headline: Silvana Armani honours a fashion dynasty with fluid, essential collection during Milan Fashion Week
Lifestyle.detail: Giorgio Armani's niece, Silvana Armani, presented her first signature collection without her uncle's input, closing Milan Fashion Week with quiet confidence. The collection was fluid and essential, honoring the Armani fashion dynasty.

Lifestyle.headline: Pope Leo XIV tells priests to avoid AI for sermon writing
Lifestyle.detail: Pope Leo XIV advised priests against using AI to write sermons, reminding them that “the brain needs to be used”. This guidance emphasizes the importance of human intellect and personal connection in delivering religious messages.

Lifestyle.headline: Inspired by sister Mukti, Neeti Mohan pushes herself to hold 'tough' planks: '4:20 seconds'
Lifestyle.detail: Singer Neeti Mohan, inspired by her sister Mukti, is pushing her physical limits by holding planks for extended durations. This showcases the influence of family support in achieving fitness goals and maintaining a healthy lifestyle.

Lifestyle.headline: The 'bite and sip' method: Macrobiotic coach shares viral hack to cut down sugar in tea
Lifestyle.detail: A macrobiotic coach has shared a viral 'bite and sip' method to reduce sugar consumption in tea. This simple technique is gaining traction as a practical approach to healthier beverage habits.

Lifestyle.headline: Out-of-control airline passenger caught on video ripping into flight crew in 'sad' meltdown
Lifestyle.detail: A video has surfaced showing an unruly airline passenger berating the flight crew, resulting in what's described as a 'sad' meltdown. This incident highlights the challenges faced by airline staff in managing disruptive passenger behavior.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Stop the spiral
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The latest escalation in the United States-Israeli strikes on Iran are a direct threat to Indonesian lives, to regional stability and to an already fragile global economy, Jakarta should focus on protection of its citizens and economic lifelines.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — March 2, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This is a collection of letters to the editor, discussing various topics including the Israel-Iran conflict, the killing of Iranian leaders, and other current events.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Region-wide Middle East war threatens millions of OFWs
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial discussing the threat of a region-wide Middle East war and its potential impact on Overseas Filipino Workers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The lessons that outlive us
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author reflects on memories from past company trips to Boracay and how those experiences shaped the values of teamwork and dedication within RFM Corporation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Development is its own form of hard power
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Alexander De Croo argues that international development is not just soft power, but a crucial form of hard power and a preemptive strike against future threats, essential for building strong partnerships and resilient systems.

World.headline: Conflict spreads as IDF strikes Hezbollah in Beirut after Iran-backed group launches attack on Israel
World.detail: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) has struck Hezbollah in Beirut following rocket attacks on Israel, further escalating the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Trump says strikes on Iran will continue as Tehran hits US assets in Gulf
World.detail: Donald Trump announced that strikes on Iran would continue after Tehran targeted US assets in the Gulf region.

World.headline: U.S. military says 1,000 targets hit in Iran, 3 American troops killed; Tehran retaliates
World.detail: The U.S. military reported hitting 1,000 targets in Iran, while also confirming that three American troops were killed during Tehran's retaliatory actions.

World.headline: Missiles strike Bahrain, UAE as Iran retaliates for US-Israel assault
World.detail: As Iran retaliates for the US-Israeli strikes, missiles have hit Bahrain and the UAE.

World.headline: Iran's Supreme Leader Killed in U.S.-Israeli Strikes
World.detail: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes, marking a significant escalation in the region.

Zimbabwe.headline: Two Harare CID Homicide Detectives Shot Dead In Zaka, Killer On The Run
Zimbabwe.detail: Two detectives from the CID Homicide department in Harare were shot and killed in Zaka, and the suspect is currently at large.

Zimbabwe.headline: Madhuku & NCA Officials Attacked In Harare: Police Accused Of Assault
Zimbabwe.detail: Madhuku and officials from the NCA were reportedly attacked in Harare, with accusations being made against the police for the assault.

Zimbabwe.headline: Caught Red-Handed: Zimbabwe Finally Explains Why It Had To Ban All Raw Mineral & Lithium Exports
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has provided an explanation for its recent ban on the export of all raw minerals and lithium, stating it was necessary to prevent exploitation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chatunga Mugabe Was Living In South Africa Illegally After Previously Being Denied Entry: Report
Zimbabwe.detail: According to reports, Chatunga Mugabe, son of the late former President Robert Mugabe, was residing in South Africa illegally after having been previously denied entry into the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZIMURA Is Dying: Alick Macheso Threatens To Quit Over Funeral Lies & Unfair Treatment Of Musicians
Zimbabwe.detail: Alick Macheso is threatening to leave ZIMURA due to alleged lies regarding funeral arrangements and unfair treatment of musicians.
